environment.scope: Fcitx, IPC, rendering, and Zenzai excluded; backend timing and RSS measurement scopes differ

fcitx_mozkey.dictionary: standard Mozc OSS dictionary from a clean checkout

performance.workload: 15 cases x 100 conversions x 5 process runs
performance.comparability: reference only; backend latency and RSS values are not directly comparable

hazkey.measurement_scope: converter-call latency samples emitted by ABProbe

rss_reference.method: maximum of ABProbe before/after RSS snapshots

fcitx_mozkey.measurement_scope: CLI process wall clock from /usr/bin/time, including startup, data loading, and test-harness overhead

limitations: The two backends use different engine-specific benchmark executables and measurement boundaries; latency and RSS ratios are intentionally not reported.
The fcitx-mozkey clean checkout does not contain the author's generated daily dictionary.
fcitx-mozkey Top-K candidate lists were not captured, so only Top-1 is compared.
The 15-case corpus is a decision spike, not a representative language benchmark.
